sutta: the buddha said monks if a word have 5 marks it is well spoken not ill spoken nor is it blame worthy nor blameable by the wise it is spoken in season or timely it is spoken in truth it is spoken softly it is spoken about the beneficial it is spoken with the heart of goodwill verily monks if a word have these 5 marks it is well spoken not ill spoken nor is it blame worthy nor blameable by the wise that's the end of the suta
commentary: here the buddha is saying that good speech have 5 qualities the first 1 it is timely spoken very timely sometimes we have something to say and sometimes to advise somebody but we must know the proper time to tell that person if that person is very upset or very angry and we choose that time to tell that person that person might not accept it so speaking in the right time is important the second 1 what you speak should be truthful should be truthful the third 1 it should be spoken gently uh not uh harshly spoken gently and then people can accept it better the fourth 1 it should be beneficial and the fifth 1 is will be spoken with the heart of goodwill with a good intention these are the marks of a good speech the next futon is
